references.title: Comparative Analysis of Clustering Algorithms and Moodle Plugin for Creation of Student Heterogeneous Groups in Online University Courses

extracted.Features: [Accesses to platform, Video tutorials watched, video viewing frequency, Videos watched, Display Frequency of Video Experiments, Assignment completion]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The authors condensed the logs into features - video watches have been aggregated. 

Data was normalized.
extracted.Models: k-Means, Mean-Shif, Hierarchical Clustering, DBSCAN, GMM, SOM
extracted.Cluster Description:
Cluster 0 - Students with low activity on the platform: Lowest average grade (53/100)

Cluster 1 - Average Activity on the platform: 2nd highest average grade (72.04)

Cluster 3 - Very Active Students who completed all assignments. Highest average grade: 82.5
extracted.Implications:
Authors subscribe to the school of heterogenous groups being better than homogenous groups. 

More engagement seems to correlate well with performance.
extracted.Comments:
Authors perform a 2-step study:

1. Comparison of clustering approaches to select the best clustering algorithm.

2. Formation of heterogenous groups using the clustering results. 

Authors find k-means with 3 groups to be the best algorithm for this task. Cluster interpretation is consistent with literature: more engagement leads to better performance overall.
extracted.Limitations:
Does not showcase results from the group formation.

Small sample size.

Online only context

annotations.text: Group work is a common strategy employed to improve learning outcomes and common strategies is random formation. The work proposes a Moodle Plugin that automates group foprmation using ML.

The plugin uses clustering to identify homogenous groups that share “similare behaviours”. Then, selection of members from different groups is done in order to ensure heterogeneity. Abstract makes no mention of empyrical results.
